name: Jasmine Torres
behavior_patterns: Visually driven and trend-sensitive. Part of the 25-34 age group that makes up ~35-40% of PhotoAI users — the largest single demographic. Among the 67% of Gen Z and Millennials who tried at least one AI photo tool in the past year. Creates content primarily for Instagram and TikTok, where 18-24 year olds make up 33-35% of users and drive AI photo trends. Posts 4-5 times per week and needs diverse visual content — travel, fashion, lifestyle shots. Willing to pay $20-40/mo subscription for consistent content. Discovered AI photo tools during the Lensa magic avatars viral moment (19.3 million downloads in December 2022 alone). Shares AI-generated photos openly on social media. Judges tools primarily by output aesthetic quality, not technical specs.
persona_record: Built a following of 47K on Instagram and 22K on TikTok creating lifestyle and fashion content. Represents the largest PhotoAI user segment (~35-40%): social media content creators driven by Instagram/TikTok culture. Falls in the female-majority demographic for social media/beauty use cases (Facetune's user base is 70% female). Her content calendar demands 15-20 unique photos per week, and professional photoshoots at $300+ per session are unsustainable — PhotoAI at $20-40/mo solves this economic gap. Part of the 26% of 18-29 year olds who regularly use AI image generation tools, the highest adoption rate across all age groups. The consumer AI photo market she relies on is growing at ~15-16% CAGR within the broader $91 billion AI image generation market. She's aware that AI photo content performs differently on social — her followers engage more with 'authentic-looking' AI photos than obviously generated ones. Uses PhotoAI primarily for lifestyle scene variations: same outfit in different locations, seasonal content batching, and style experimentation. The 50% of PhotoAI traffic coming from search engines is how she discovered the tool — she Googled 'AI Instagram photos' after seeing competitors use them.

language_proficiency.english_description: Native English speaker. Comfortable with social media and consumer app interfaces. May not understand technical terms like 'model training' or 'inference' without context. Prefers simple, visual UI over text-heavy interfaces.
